name: Hotkey Bar
description: Function key shortcut bar inspired by DOS/Norton Commander style interfaces.

standard.description: Standard F1-F10 function key bar with evenly spaced shortcut labels in a fixed bottom row

visual_brief: Fixed horizontal bar at the bottom of the viewport displaying function key shortcuts in a DOS/Norton Commander style. Each slot shows a function key label (F1, F2, ... F10) in a highlighted badge followed by the action name (Help, Menu, View, Edit, Copy, Move, Mkdir, Delete, etc.). Background is typically dark with bright key labels. Extended preset adds Shift+F and Ctrl+F combinations as additional rows. The bar spans the full viewport width with evenly distributed key slots.

responsive.mobile: Bar shows only the most critical shortcuts (F1-F5). Remaining keys accessible via a more button. Key labels use abbreviated action names.
responsive.tablet: Full F1-F10 bar visible. Key slots slightly narrower.
responsive.desktop: Full bar with all function keys and comfortable spacing. Extended preset shows additional modifier combinations.

layout_hints.bar_priority: The key label should read before the action text so users can scan function bindings rapidly.
layout_hints.density: Keep the bar extremely compact and horizontal. It is terminal chrome, not a secondary navigation rail.
layout_hints.mobile_translation: On smaller screens, preserve the most important bindings and collapse the rest rather than shrinking every slot to illegibility.

motion.micro: Hovered or active key slots may brighten slightly to acknowledge interaction without looking like conventional buttons.
motion.transitions: The bar should feel stable and ever-present; avoid animated reflow unless shortcut sets genuinely change.

keyboard: Shortcut items should be reachable in sequence, with the underlying key command still working even if the bar itself is not focused.
announcements: Each binding should expose both the function key and its action name semantically so the bar is understandable without color-coded badges alone.
